name: anzecc-anzg-ssd-data
title: ANZECC/ANZG Species Sensitivity Distribution Data
description: Raw toxicity data and analyte metadata for fitting species sensitivity distributions (SSDs) to derive ANZECC 2000 / ANZG freshwater guideline values. Intended for use in msPAF calculations. Data sourced from Warne (2000) 'Derivation of the ANZECC and ARMCANZ Water Quality Guidelines for Toxicants' and ANZG (2021-2026) technical brief XLSX files.

licenses.name: odc-pddl
licenses.title: Open Data Commons Public Domain Dedication and License

sources.title: Warne (2000) — Description of how each toxicant trigger value was derived
sources.description: Primary source for ANZECC 2000 SSD data. All values in µg/L unless otherwise noted.

sources.title: ANZG Technical Briefs (2021-2026)
sources.description: XLSX data-entry files for NH3-N (2026), NO3-N (2025), B (2021), Cr-III (2026), Cu (2023 draft), Ni (2024 draft), Zn (2024 draft).

resources.name: observations
resources.title: ANZECC 2000 raw toxicity observations (Warne 2000)
resources.description: One row per toxicity value used by Warne (2000) in SSD fitting. All concentrations in µg/L. For ANZG XLSX analytes (NH3-N, NO3-N, B, Cr, Cu, Ni, Zn) the canonical data source is the XLSX file — see anzecc_analyte_metadata.csv.

fields.name: analyte
fields.description: Analyte name. Matches observations_analyte in anzecc_analyte_metadata.csv. g-BHC has two entries ('g-BHC_chronic' and 'g-BHC_acute') because Warne fitted both.

fields.name: value_ug_L
fields.description: Toxicity threshold concentration in µg/L as reported by Warne (2000). For MR analytes this is an acute LC50/EC50; for HR analytes a chronic NOEC/LOEC; for BaP a QSAR-predicted value.

fields.name: endpoint
fields.description: Type of toxicity endpoint.

fields.name: species_id
fields.description: Placeholder species identifier (Sp_001, Sp_002, …). Species names were not recorded by Warne (2000) in the PDF extraction. One value per species as Warne used the per-species minimum.

fields.name: source_doc
fields.description: Source document identifier.

fields.name: source_page
fields.description: PDF page number in Warne (2000) where this data appears. Empty if page not confirmed.

fields.name: verified
fields.description: TRUE if the value was read directly from the Warne (2000) PDF during the current extraction session. FALSE if it was extracted in an earlier session without full page-level verification.

fields.name: notes
fields.description: Extraction notes, method caveats, or data quality flags. Populated on the first row for each analyte only.

resources.name: analyte_metadata
resources.title: ANZECC/ANZG analyte metadata
resources.description: One row per analyte. Captures reliability classification, ACR, ANZECC-adopted percentile, published DGVs at all four protection levels, and fit parameters needed to reproduce ANZECC trigger values from the raw observations.

fields.name: analyte
fields.description: Canonical analyte name matching guidelineDF and analyteDF in the dashboard.

fields.name: anzecc_reliability
fields.description: ANZECC 2000 reliability tier.

fields.name: anzecc_data_type
fields.description: Type of toxicity data used in the ANZECC SSD.

fields.name: ssd_available
fields.description: TRUE if an SSD was fitted (or can be fitted) for this analyte.

fields.name: no_ssd_reason
fields.description: Reason no SSD is available (for ssd_available=FALSE rows).

fields.name: acr
fields.description: Acute-to-Chronic Ratio used by ANZECC for MR analytes. NA for HR, LR, QSAR analytes. Divide the fitted acute HC by this value to obtain the chronic-equivalent trigger.

fields.name: anzecc_hc_percentile
fields.description: Which HC percentile ANZECC used as the basis for the trigger value. Normally 5 (HC5 = 95% species protection). Set to 1 (HC1 = 99% protection) for analytes where ANZECC chose the more conservative HC1 due to bioaccumulation potential or acute toxicity concerns (Se, DDT, BaP, Azinphos-methyl).

fields.name: trigger_divisor
fields.description: Divisor applied to the HC (at anzecc_hc_percentile) to obtain the published DGV. For HR analytes = 1 (no division). For MR analytes = ACR. For LR/QSAR analytes = assessment factor (typically 10). Formula: DGV_95pct = HC(anzecc_hc_percentile) / trigger_divisor.

fields.name: n_min_override
fields.description: Override for the minimum n threshold in fit_ssd_models.R (default 6). Set to 5 for Se, which Warne fitted with n=5 including an outlier.

fields.name: fit_dist
fields.description: ssdtools distribution name(s) to use when fitting the SSD for this analyte. A single name (e.g. 'burrIII3', 'lnorm') fits only that distribution. A comma-separated list (e.g. 'lnorm,llogis,gamma') fits all listed distributions and model-averages. Empty/NA means use the global --dists CLI default. Warne 2000 analytes use 'burrIII3' (Burr Type III — the distribution Warne 2000 used throughout). ANZG XLSX analytes use 'lnorm'.

fields.name: dgv_99pct_ug_L
fields.description: Published ANZECC 2000 guideline value at 99% species protection (HC1-equivalent) in µg/L. Sourced from guidelineDF.qs (converted from mg/L). Empty if not available (LR analytes have one value only). Note: BaP stores its single published DGV here (0.1 µg/L = HC1÷10 bioaccumulation factor) even though guidelineDF placed it in the 90% column — the 99% column is the correct placement because the DGV is derived from HC1 (99% species protection level).

fields.name: dgv_95pct_ug_L
fields.description: Published ANZECC 2000 guideline value at 95% species protection (HC5-equivalent) in µg/L. This is the primary DGV used for compliance assessment.

fields.name: dgv_90pct_ug_L
fields.description: Published ANZECC 2000 guideline value at 90% species protection (HC10-equivalent) in µg/L.

fields.name: dgv_80pct_ug_L
fields.description: Published ANZECC 2000 guideline value at 80% species protection (HC20-equivalent) in µg/L.

fields.name: dgv_conditions
fields.description: Chemical or physical conditions under which the DGV applies (e.g. pH, hardness, DOC). Important for ammonia (pH/temperature dependent), metals with hardness corrections, and BLM-adjusted metals.

fields.name: observations_analyte
fields.description: Key into the 'analyte' column of anzecc_warne2000_observations.csv to use when fitting this analyte's SSD. May differ from 'analyte' (e.g. g-BHC -> 'g-BHC_acute'; 4.4-DDT -> '4,4\'-DDT'). Empty for ANZG XLSX analytes.

fields.name: data_source
fields.description: Where the raw observations come from.

fields.name: source_doc
fields.description: Filename of the source document.

fields.name: source_page
fields.description: Page or sheet reference within the source document.

fields.name: notes
fields.description: Data quality flags, extraction caveats, and comparison notes.
